questions.question: What is the primary architectural penalty of attempting to run massive analytical queries across a traditional 'Row-Based' database (like PostgreSQL)?
options: Row-based databases natively prevent SQL aggregation functions.

Row-based storage forces the database to read massive, unnecessary amounts of irrelevant data off the physical hard drive (like names and emails), even if the analytical query only explicitly requests a single integer column.
Row-based storage encrypts the data arrays sequentially.
Row-based storage strictly relies on standard CSS frameworks natively.
questions.answer: Row-based storage forces the database to read massive, unnecessary amounts of irrelevant data off the physical hard drive (like names and emails), even if the analytical query only explicitly requests a single integer column.
questions.explanation: To calculate the sum of total sales, a row-based database reads every single string and date physically interspersed around the numbers.

questions.question: How does 'Columnar Storage' (e.g., Apache Parquet) explicitly resolve the grueling Read-Speed bottlenecks of massive Data Warehouses?
options: It translates SQL queries into lightweight Javascript React APIs natively.

It physically reorganizes data on the hard drive, clustering all identical values for a specific column (like 'prices') sequentially together in a single block. This allows the engine to explicitly skip loading useless columns entirely during analytical queries.
It dynamically stores all data securely within high-speed GPU caches.
It deletes missing NULL values safely and dynamically.
questions.answer: It physically reorganizes data on the hard drive, clustering all identical values for a specific column (like 'prices') sequentially together in a single block. This allows the engine to explicitly skip loading useless columns entirely during analytical queries.
questions.explanation: If your table has 50 columns, but your query only selects 2, Columnar strictly only reads those 2 columns physically from disk.

questions.question: Why do Columnar Storage formats mathematically achieve vastly superior file compression ratios compared to standard Row-Based formats?
options: Because Columnar formats automatically delete whitespace from strings cleanly.

Because data types reside exclusively together (e.g., millions of consecutive identical integers), highly efficient aggressive compression algorithms like Run-Length Encoding can physically compress the data exponentially better.
Because Columnar formats compress files securely inside ZIP folders natively.
Because Columnar formats actively ignore all JSON dictionaries cleanly.
questions.answer: Because data types reside exclusively together (e.g., millions of consecutive identical integers), highly efficient aggressive compression algorithms like Run-Length Encoding can physically compress the data exponentially better.
questions.explanation: You cannot efficiently compress a row containing a string, an integer, and a date. You can effectively compress 1 million identical integers stored back-to-back.
